title: Multimodal AI for Autism Assessment: Bayesian Uncertainty in Clinical ADOS
desc: A late-fusion Bayesian ensemble pipeline that processes gaze-tracking, skeletal pose, and prosodic audio to generate ADOS-2-aligned risk scores—while mathematically preserving clinical doubt. Features an interactive workbench demonstrating how conflicting behavioral telemetry forces sub-model divergence and uncertainty quantification.

title: How I Accidentally Wrote a Research Paper About My Own Playlist
desc: This was supposed to be one line on my About page. I was going to embed a Spotify playlist, write something like 'this helps me focus,' and move on with my life. But I have ADHD, and I am a psychologist, and I am constitutionally incapable of encountering a behavioral phenomenon without pulling it apart to figure out why it works.

title: Natural Language Autoencoders & Latent Evaluation Awareness
desc: The challenge of AI alignment has always been fundamentally tied to the problem of opacity. This case study details Anthropic's NLA architecture, exploring how white-box interpretability tools can extract unverbalized evaluation awareness.

title: The Tokenization Trap: Why Logographic Languages Don't Save Agentic Context
desc: An investigation into the "vibe coding" hypothesis: why prompting agents in Simplified Chinese fails to reduce token costs due to BPE fragmentation, and the hidden tax of translation overhead in agentic workflows.

title: Is Using Simplified Chinese an Efficient Token-Saving and Context-Saving Approach for Agentic Workflows?
desc: Large language models (LLMs) deployed in agentic workflows must autonomously consume, process, and retain significant amounts of information. As these workflows become more complex, managing context window limits and the associated token costs has emerged as a primary engineering challenge.
